transcript_facts:
- [Assessment] yeah , so , um , my impression of you at this time , um , for your high blood sugars , that you do have this diagnosis of diabetes .
- [Findings] so , hannah is a , uh , 52-year-old female here for evaluation of a high blood sugar .
- [Findings] they gave me some fluids and they told me just to try to really cut out eating any extra sweets and get back into my normal routine and diet, but i am still staying with my family, and my mom and grandmother are big bakers so i think it will be really hard to get into a routine there so i just want to make sure i am nipping this high blood sugar thing in the bud.
- [Findings] so let's go ahead and do a quick physical exam .
- [Findings] show me the blood sugar . show me the blood glucose . show me the hemoglobin a1c . okay , so i see here that both your blood sugar and your hemoglobin a1c are elevated . so your blood sugars have probably been running high for probably a few months .
- [Findings] show me the diabetes labs .
- [Findings] even patients in , you know , in their mid-twenties can develop type i diabetes , so we're gon na go off and , we're gon na go ahead and , and send some labs to work that up . order a complete metabolic panel . that will help keep your blood sugars down .
- [Findings] and then if some of the autoimmune labs that come back show that you have an autoimmune disorder , meaning that you would have type i diabetes , then we would have to go ahead and put you on insulin .
- [Findings] um , and the , the nurse will come in and she'll give you a glucometer and teach you how to do blood sugar testing .
- [History of Present Illness] um , i have high blood sugar . yeah , osteoarthritis , arterial fibrillation , and reflux .
- [History of Present Illness] i heard you were in the emergency room with a high blood sugar .
- [History of Present Illness] in the beginning i felt fine, but i have started to feel it after a couple of weeks, and this past week i just felt really light-headed and i could ... was seeing spots , really dizzy . so i went into the emergency room and they said i had a high blood sugar .
- [History of Present Illness] so you have had any fever or chills ? any chest pain or shortness of breath ? abdominal pain ? any burning when you urinate ? any joint pain or muscle aches ?
- [History of Present Illness] i do have osteoarthritis though , so that , when i say , " no , " it's not , like , nothing out of the norm-
- [History of Present Illness] um , and then , i know that you have this history of a-fib , atrial fibrillation . only when i'm really anxious .
- [History of Present Illness] and how are you doing with your reflux ?
- [History of Present Illness] i see here you're on the protonix , 40 milligrams , once a day .
- [History of Present Illness] so , listening to you , um , look , or looking at you , you appear in no distress . your thyroid is a little enlarged , but non-tender . there's no carotid bruits , your heart is in irregular rate and rhythm , and your lungs are clear . your abdomen is nice and soft , and you have no edema in your lower extremities .
- [History of Present Illness] and then , from an atrial fibrillation standpoint , your heart , even though you're in atrial fibrillation right now , is in a good rate .
- [History of Present Illness] and for your reflux , we'll just continue you on the protonix , okay ?
- [Plan] i've been taking the medication that you prescribed to me and that's been helping a lot .
- [Plan] and i'd like to start you on metformin , 500 milligrams , twice a day .
- [Plan] order metformin , 500 milligrams , twice a day .
- [Plan] and we'll just continue you on your metoprolol , 25 milligrams , twice a day .
- [Plan] order a refill on metoprolol , 25 milligrams , twice a day .
